message:
chore(beads): seed 5 fresh [HIGH] beads after backlog drain

- r71v: redis7 surface reality-check (CLUSTER, ACL selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID)
- r72v: RESP3 protocol parity (HELLO 3 push frames, map/set/double/bignum)
- r73v: RDB+AOF file-level round-trip vs upstream (corpus + LZF + version header)
- r74v: pub/sub scalability + keyspace notifications (sharded pub/sub, K/E mask)
- r75v: Lua scripting + FUNCTION audit (EVAL, EVAL_RO, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE, redis.*)

Created via direct JSONL append (DB-JSONL drift means br create silently
no-ops on this checkout). All five are HIGH/P1/bug, labelled, with
specific scope and conformance-harness application notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-1pe7","title":"[HIGH] DEBUG DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE/OBJECT parity — 48 divergences across digest-computation + DEBUG OBJECT encoding/addr/serialize fields","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime (48 cases after enable-debug-command yes): (a) DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE — fr-runtime doesn't implement the xor-SHA1-of-per-key-digest algorithm upstream uses, so every digest case diverges. (b) DEBUG OBJECT — upstream reply is 'Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<enc> serializedlength:N lru:... lru_seconds_idle:N type:<type>'; ours emits a shorter 'refcount:1 encoding:<enc>' without the pointer/lru/serialized fields. (c) DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE invalid-value / wrong-arity wording differs. (d) Case-insensitive DEBUG subcommand dispatch — upstream normalises, ours may not. Fix: wire DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE in fr-runtime + fill in the full DEBUG OBJECT reply + align SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE error wording + lowercase dispatch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T13:25:45.276247292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:13:00Z","close_reason":"Already resolved: live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime passes 100% under STRICT mode. DEBUG OBJECT + DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE canonicalizers + SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE permissive parse + upstream-wording fixes all landed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-1qdg","title":"Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands","description":"# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1v8b","title":"Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake","description":"# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1zxx","title":"Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline","description":"# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.224067917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:56:05.922840524Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:56:05.922656586Z","close_reason":"Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.702954088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","title":"Audit SCAN/KEYS/SORT ordering assumptions before BTreeMap→HashMap migration","description":"# Audit Ordering Assumptions

## What

Before replacing BTreeMap with HashMap in Store::entries, systematically identify
every code path that depends on BTreeMap's sorted iteration order. These must be
adapted for HashMap's non-deterministic iteration.

## Specific Areas to Audit

### 1. SCAN / HSCAN / SSCAN / ZSCAN
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — search for scan-related functions
- Current behavior: likely iterates BTreeMap in sorted order with a cursor
  that's a key-position offset
- Redis behavior: uses reversed-bit-increment cursor over hash bucket indices;
  no ordering guarantee; may return duplicates; guarantees full coverage of
  keys that exist throughout the entire scan
- Action needed: re-implement cursor as either epoch-based (last-seen-key) or
  snapshot-based

### 2. KEYS pattern matching
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — search for keys_matching or similar
- Current behavior: returns keys in BTreeMap sorted order
- Redis behavior: no ordering guarantee
- Action needed: verify no conformance tests depend on sorted KEYS output;
  if they do, mark as over-specified and fix tests

### 3. SORT command with BY nosort
- Location: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs — SORT handler
- Current behavior: NOSORT returns elements in "natural order"
- Redis behavior: for lists, NOSORT returns insertion order (list-internal);
  for sets, returns arbitrary order; doesn't depend on entries map order
- Action needed: verify SORT doesn't use entries iteration order

### 4. state_digest computation
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:7875
- Current behavior: iterates entries in sorted order, chains FNV hashes
- Action needed: MUST change to order-independent algorithm (XOR-of-per-entry-
  hashes) regardless. This is also a prerequisite for frankenredis-n5xv.
- Note: state_digest is now on the cold path (lazy-digest refactor), so the
  algorithm change is safe; the only concern is digest stability for conformance
  fixtures that check evidence-ledger digests. Search for hard-coded digest
  values in golden_outputs/ and conformance fixtures.

### 5. DEBUG / OBJECT / internal dump paths
- Any path that iterates all keys and depends on order

### 6. Conformance fixtures
- Search golden_outputs/ for any fixture that asserts key ordering
- Check if any conformance test sorts keys differently or depends on
  deterministic iteration

## Deliverable

A markdown checklist of every ordering-dependent path, with the required
adaptation strategy for each. This checklist becomes the implementation
guide for the migration subtask.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Complete audit document covering all 6 areas above
2. No unidentified ordering dependency in the codebase
3. Each dependency has a named adaptation strategy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:33.024141262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:00.602794686Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:00.602582915Z","close_reason":"Audit completed; ordering dependencies documented and implementation strategy chosen","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:33.024141262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Audit summary: deterministic ordering dependencies are confirmed in runtime KEYS/SCAN, store active-expire cursoring, state_digest, RDB snapshot export via all_keys(), and unit/conformance tests that hard-code sorted KEYS/SCAN output. Implementation path: HashMap for Store::entries plus an auxiliary ordered key index to preserve deterministic cold-path behavior while moving hot-path lookups off BTreeMap.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:38:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","title":"Implement HashMap migration for Store::entries","description":"# Implement HashMap Migration

## What

Replace BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Entry> with HashMap<Vec<u8>, Entry> at
crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:772 and adapt all ordering-dependent code paths
identified in the audit subtask (frankenredis-hs04.1).

## Key Implementation Steps

### 1. Change the type declaration
```rust
// Before:
entries: BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Entry>,

// After:
entries: HashMap<Vec<u8>, Entry>,
```

### 2. Fix SCAN cursor
Implement epoch-based cursor strategy:
- SCAN 0 starts a new iteration, returns a batch of N keys + next cursor
- Cursor encodes the iteration state (e.g., hash of last-seen key + count)
- On SCAN <cursor>, find position and return next batch
- Guarantee: all keys present throughout full scan are returned (may duplicate)

### 3. Fix state_digest
Change from sequential FNV chain to XOR-of-per-entry-hashes:
```rust
pub fn state_digest(&self) -> String {
    let mut hash = 0_u64;
    for (key, entry) in &self.entries {
        hash ^= entry_hash(key, entry);
    }
    format!("{hash:016x}")
}
```

### 4. Fix any test assertions
Update tests that depend on sorted key iteration order.

### 5. Consider HashMap capacity hints
Pre-size the HashMap based on expected keyspace. The default load factor
(7/8 for std HashMap) is fine; don't over-optimize.

## Files to Change

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — primary change (entries type + all methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs — if any iteration depends on order
- golden_outputs/ — if any fixtures have order-dependent expected output

## This Must Come After

The audit subtask (frankenredis-hs04.1) must complete first to identify all
adaptation points. Implementing without the audit risks silent ordering bugs
that only surface in production under specific key distributions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.507423683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:44.135030120Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:44.134737927Z","close_reason":"Implemented and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.507423683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:27.473696763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation started. Store::entries now uses HashMap with a parallel ordered key index reserved for deterministic global-iteration paths (KEYS/SCAN helpers, active-expire cursoring, state_digest, snapshot/export helpers). Remote fr-store validation passed, and remote workspace check completed successfully before artifact retrieval tailed.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:25Z"},{"id":6,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation started. Store::entries now uses HashMap with a parallel ordered key index reserved for deterministic global-iteration paths (KEYS/SCAN helpers, active-expire cursoring, state_digest, snapshot/export helpers). Remote  passed, and remote workspace cargo check completed successfully before artifact retrieval tailed.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:38Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation complete: Store::entries now uses HashMap with a BTreeSet ordered index for deterministic global iteration. Validation passed via rch-offloaded cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --check passed locally.","created_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","title":"Benchmark + conformance gate for HashMap migration","description":"# Benchmark + Conformance Gate for HashMap Migration

## What

After frankenredis-hs04.2 (HashMap implementation) is complete, run the full
quality gate and produce a before/after delta artifact.

## Steps

1. Build release binary with HashMap changes
2. Run fr-bench for all workloads: set, get, incr, mixed (p1 and p16)
3. Capture perf flamegraph to confirm BTreeMap::insert and memcmp overhead
   is reduced
4. Run cargo test --workspace (all unit tests)
5. Run cargo test -p fr-conformance (all 43 conformance tests)
6. Verify SCAN works correctly with a multi-cursor full-scan test
7. Compare throughput numbers to the April 9 baseline
8. Write isomorphism proof artifact at
   artifacts/optimization/hashmap-migration/ISOMORPHISM_PROOF.md

## Expected Outcome

BTreeMap::insert (2.93%) and __memcmp_avx2_movbe (12.73%) should both drop
significantly. Combined ~15% CPU savings → estimated 10-15% throughput
improvement. SET p1 should move from 75K to ~85K+ ops/sec.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 43 conformance tests pass
2. All workspace tests pass
3. Benchmark shows measurable improvement (>5% on SET)
4. Flamegraph confirms BTreeMap is no longer in top 10 CPU consumers
5. Isomorphism proof artifact is written and checked in
6. README updated with new numbers if improvement is significant","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.479134166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.478867962Z","close_reason":"Benchmark verified: SET p1=66K (70% Redis), SET p16=257K (30% Redis), all 43 conformance tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:27.573416302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-hx4n","title":"Add structured fr-protocol fuzz target for parser limits and RESP frame sequences","description":"Evidence: fuzz_resp_parser.rs currently throws raw bytes at parse_frame/parse_frame_with_config, and fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs only checks encode/decode identity. That leaves a gap around structure-aware parser-limit boundaries and multi-frame consumed-length invariants in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:128 (parse_frame), 132 (parse_frame_with_config), 140 (parse_frame_internal), 180 (parse_bulk), and 211 (parse_array).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware libFuzzer target that generates valid and near-valid RESP scalars/arrays plus trailing frame sequences, varies ParserConfig limits, and asserts invariants: consumed bytes stop exactly at the first frame, accepted frames stay accepted under looser limits, rejected frames at tight limits never panic, and back-to-back encoded frames can be iteratively parsed without drift.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_configured_sequences.rs hitting fr-protocol directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:17:19.110653598Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-protocol","fuzz","testing"]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-zub","title":"Fix LTRIM and LRANGE index normalization bugs","description":"LTRIM and LRANGE incorrectly handle stop indices that are less than start or less than -len, returning or keeping the first element instead of an empty list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:15:20.278699584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.164920407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.162202704Z","close_reason":"Fixed LTRIM and LRANGE: clamp normalized indices before comparison to prevent negative-to-usize wrapping when indices are out of range","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r71v","title":"[HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED","description":"Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: spawn vendored redis-server, send each combination via the existing live-oracle harness, surface every divergence as a follow-up [HIGH] bead. +{"id":"frankenredis-r72v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers","description":"Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["resp3","protocol","hello","push"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["rdb","aof","persistence","roundtrip"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r74v","title":"[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks","description":"(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pubsub","scalability","keyspace-notifications"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r75v","title":"[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface","description":"Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.
